records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2644 - Imran al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"A Muhrim may tie a cloth around his waist or secure it around the position of the loincloth (izar), but he should not raise it to his chest."
records.arabicText: 2644 - وَ رَوَى عِمْرَانُ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْمُحْرِمُ يَشُدُّ عَلَى بَطْنِهِ اَلْعِمَامَةَ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ يُعَصِّبُهَا عَلَى مَوْضِعِ اَلْإِزَارِ وَ لاَ يَرْفَعُهَا إِلَى صَدْرِهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2644 - Imran al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A Muhrim may tie a cloth around his waist or secure it around the position of the loincloth (izar), but he should not raise it to his chest."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2645 - Ibn Faddal narrated from Yunus ibn Ya'qub, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Muhrim tying a belt (himyan) around his waist.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, and what is better than securing his money?"
records.arabicText: 2645 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ يَعْقُوبَ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَشُدُّ اَلْهِمْيَانَ فِي وَسَطِهِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ مَا خَيْرُهُ بَعْدَ نَفَقَتِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2645 - Ibn Faddal narrated from Yunus ibn Ya'qub, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Muhrim tying a belt (himyan) around his waist.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Yes, and what is better than securing his money?"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2646 - In a narration from Abu Basir, from Imam (as), he said:
"My father (as) used to tie his money securely around his waist, as it was part of completing his Hajj."
records.arabicText: 2646 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَشُدُّ عَلَى بَطْنِهِ نَفَقَتَهُ يَسْتَوْثِقُ بِهَا فَإِنَّهَا تَمَامُ حَجِّهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2646 - In a narration from Abu Basir, from Imam (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father (as) used to tie his money securely around his waist, as it was part of completing his Hajj."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2647 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm for a Muhrim to use kohl that does not contain musk or camphor if he has an eye ailment. A woman in Ihram may use any kohl except black kohl intended for adornment."
records.arabicText: 2647 - رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ لِلْمُحْرِمِ أَنْ يَكْتَحِلَ بِكُحْلٍ لَيْسَ فِيهِ مِسْكٌ وَ لاَ كَافُورٌ إِذَا اِشْتَكَى عَيْنَيْهِ وَ تَكْتَحِلُ اَلْمَرْأَةُ اَلْمُحْرِمَةُ بِالْكُحْلِ كُلِّهِ إِلاَّ كُحْلاً أَسْوَدَ لِزِينَةٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2647 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm for a Muhrim to use kohl that does not contain musk or camphor if he has an eye ailment. A woman in Ihram may use any kohl except black kohl intended for adornment."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2648 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
"A Muhrim may apply kohl to his eyes if he wishes, provided it is made with sabr (aloe) and does not contain saffron (za'faran) or turmeric (wars)."
records.arabicText: 2648 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «يَكْتَحِلُ اَلْمُحْرِمُ عَيْنَيْهِ إِنْ شَاءَ بِصَبِرٍ لَيْسَ فِيهِ زَعْفَرَانٌ وَ لاَ وَرْسٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2648 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A Muhrim may apply kohl to his eyes if he wishes, provided it is made with sabr (aloe) and does not contain saffron (za'faran) or turmeric (wars)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2649 - Hariz narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Do not look into a mirror while you are in Ihram, because it is considered a form of adornment (zeenah)."
records.arabicText: 2649 - وَ رَوَى حَرِيزٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ تَنْظُرْ فِي اَلْمِرْآةِ وَ أَنْتَ مُحْرِمٌ لِأَنَّهُ مِنَ اَلزِّينَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2649 - Hariz narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not look into a mirror while you are in Ihram, because it is considered a form of adornment (zeenah)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2650 - It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Muhrim using a miswak (tooth-stick).
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
I asked: "What if it causes bleeding while using it?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, it is part of the Sunnah."
records.arabicText: 2650 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَسْتَاكُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ» قَالَ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ أَدْمَى يَسْتَاكُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ هُوَ مِنَ اَلسُّنَّةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2650 - It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Muhrim using a miswak (tooth-stick).
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
I asked: "What if it causes bleeding while using it?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, it is part of the Sunnah."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2651 - Hammad narrated from Hariz, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm for a Muhrim to undergo cupping (hijamah), provided he does not shave or pluck hair."
records.arabicText: 2651 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَحْتَجِمَ اَلْمُحْرِمُ مَا لَمْ يَحْلِقْ أَوْ يَقْلَعِ اَلشَّعْرَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2651 - Hammad narrated from Hariz, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm for a Muhrim to undergo cupping (hijamah), provided he does not shave or pluck hair."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2652 - Dharih asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Muhrim undergoing cupping (hijamah).
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if he fears harm from the blood."
records.arabicText: 2652 - وَ سَأَلَ ذَرِيحٌ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَحْتَجِمُ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ إِذَا خَشِيَ اَلدَّمَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2652 - Dharih asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Muhrim undergoing cupping (hijamah).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Yes, if he fears harm from the blood."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2653 - Al-Hasan al-Sayqal asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Muhrim whose tooth is causing him pain: "Can he have it extracted?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2653 - وَ سَأَلَ اَلْحَسَنُ اَلصَّيْقَلُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يُؤْذِيهِ ضِرْسُهُ أَ يَقْلَعُهُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2653 - Al-Hasan al-Sayqal asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Muhrim whose tooth is causing him pain: "Can he have it extracted?"
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, there is no harm in it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2654 - Imran al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about a Muhrim who has a wound and uses a medicine containing saffron.
Imam (as) said: "If the saffron is the dominant ingredient in the medicine, then no. But if the other ingredients are dominant, there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2654 - وَ رَوَى عِمْرَانُ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَكُونُ بِهِ اَلْجُرْحُ فَيَتَدَاوَى بِدَوَاءٍ فِيهِ زَعْفَرَانٌ فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ اَلزَّعْفَرَانُ غَالِباً عَلَى اَلدَّوَاءِ فَلاَ وَ إِنْ كَانَتِ اَلْأَدْوِيَةُ غَالِبَةً عَلَيْهِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2654 - Imran al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about a Muhrim who has a wound and uses a medicine containing saffron.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the saffron is the dominant ingredient in the medicine, then no. But if the other ingredients are dominant, there is no harm in it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2655 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked Imam (as) about a Muhrim squeezing a boil and tying a cloth around it.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2655 - وَ سَأَلَهُ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَعْصِرُ اَلدُّمَّلَ وَ يَرْبِطُ عَلَيْهِ اَلْخِرْقَةَ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2656 - Imam (as) said:
"If a Muhrim falls ill, let him seek treatment with what is permissible for him to consume while in the state of Ihram."
records.arabicText: 2656 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِذَا اِشْتَكَى اَلْمُحْرِمُ فَلْيَتَدَاوَ بِمَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2656 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a Muhrim falls ill, let him seek treatment with what is permissible for him to consume while in the state of Ihram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2657 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If a Muhrim develops an abscess or a boil, he may lance it and treat it with oil or fat."
records.arabicText: 2657 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا خَرَجَ بِالْمُحْرِمِ اَلْخُرَاجُ وَ اَلدُّمَّلُ فَلْيَبُطَّهُ وَ لْيُدَاوِهِ بِزَيْتٍ أَوْ سَمْنٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2657 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a Muhrim develops an abscess or a boil, he may lance it and treat it with oil or fat."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2658 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from one of the Imams (as) regarding a Muhrim whose hands have cracked.
Imam (as) said: "He may anoint them with oil, fat, or melted fat (ihalah)."
records.arabicText: 2658 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلْمُحْرِمِ تَشَقَّقُ يَدَاهُ فَقَالَ «يَدْهُنُهُمَا بِزَيْتٍ أَوْ سَمْنٍ أَوْ إِهَالَةٍ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2658 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from one of the Imams (as) regarding a Muhrim whose hands have cracked.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He may anoint them with oil, fat, or melted fat (ihalah)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2659 - Muhammad ibn al-Fudayl narrated from Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a woman who wished to enter Ihram but feared skin cracking. Could she apply henna beforehand?
Imam (as) said: "I do not like for her to do so."
records.arabicText: 2659 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي اَلصَّبَّاحِ اَلْكِنَانِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اِمْرَأَةٍ أَرَادَتْ أَنْ تُحْرِمَ فَتَخَوَّفَتِ اَلشُّقَاقَ تَخْضِبُ بِالْحِنَّاءِ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ «مَا يُعْجِبُنِي أَنْ تَفْعَلَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2659 - Muhammad ibn al-Fudayl narrated from Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a woman who wished to enter Ihram but feared skin cracking. Could she apply henna beforehand?
Imam (as) said: "I do not like for her to do so."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2660 - It is reported that Imam Ali ibn al-Husayn (as) used to say to his family when preparing for a journey to Makkah:
"Be cautious not to include anything scented, such as perfume or saffron, in our provisions, whether for us to eat or to serve to others."
records.arabicText: 2660 - وَ كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : إِذَا تَجَهَّزَ إِلَى مَكَّةَ قَالَ لِأَهْلِهِ «إِيَّاكُمْ أَنْ تَجْعَلُوا فِي زَادِنَا شَيْئاً مِنَ اَلطِّيبِ وَ لاَ اَلزَّعْفَرَانِ نَأْكُلْهُ أَوْ نُطْعِمْهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2660 - It is reported that Imam Ali ibn al-Husayn (as) used to say to his family when preparing for a journey to Makkah:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Be cautious not to include anything scented, such as perfume or saffron, in our provisions, whether for us to eat or to serve to others."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2661 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Four types of fragrances are disliked for a Muhrim: musk, ambergris, saffron, and turmeric. He also disliked fragrant oils with a strong scent."
records.arabicText: 2661 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «يُكْرَهُ مِنَ اَلطِّيبِ أَرْبَعَةُ أَشْيَاءَ لِلْمُحْرِمِ اَلْمِسْكُ وَ اَلْعَنْبَرُ وَ اَلزَّعْفَرَانُ وَ اَلْوَرْسُ وَ كَانَ يَكْرَهُ مِنَ اَلْأَدْهَانِ اَلطَّيِّبَةَ اَلرِّيحِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2661 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Four types of fragrances are disliked for a Muhrim: musk, ambergris, saffron, and turmeric. He also disliked fragrant oils with a strong scent."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2662 - It was narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Harun, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I ate a sweet dish (khabis) that contained saffron until I was full while I was in Ihram."
Imam (as) said: "When you complete your rituals and wish to leave Makkah, buy dates with a dirham and give them in charity. This will be an expiation for that and for anything else that might have affected your Ihram unknowingly."
records.arabicText: 2662 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ هَارُونَ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَكَلْتُ خَبِيصاً فِيهِ زَعْفَرَانٌ حَتَّى شَبِعْتُ مِنْهُ وَ أَنَا مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ «إِذَا فَرَغْتَ مِنْ مَنَاسِكِكَ وَ أَرَدْتَ اَلْخُرُوجَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ فَابْتَعْ بِدِرْهَمٍ تَمْراً وَ تَصَدَّقْ بِهِ فَيَكُونَ كَفَّارَةً لِذَلِكَ وَ لِمَا دَخَلَ عَلَيْكَ فِي إِحْرَامِكَ مِمَّا لاَ تَعْلَمُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2662 - It was narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Harun, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I ate a sweet dish (khabis) that contained saffron until I was full while I was in Ihram."
Imam (as) said: "When you complete your rituals and wish to leave Makkah, buy dates with a dirham and give them in charity. This will be an expiation for that and for anything else that might have affected your Ihram unknowingly."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2663 - Zurara narrated from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
"Whoever intentionally eats saffron or food containing fragrance while in Ihram must offer a blood sacrifice (dam). However, if he does so out of forgetfulness, there is nothing upon him, but he should seek forgiveness from Allah (swt) and repent to Him."
records.arabicText: 2663 - وَ رَوَى زُرَارَةُ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ أَكَلَ زَعْفَرَاناً مُتَعَمِّداً أَوْ طَعَاماً فِيهِ طِيبٌ فَعَلَيْهِ دَمٌ وَ إِنْ كَانَ نَاسِياً فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ وَ يَسْتَغْفِرُ اَللَّهَ وَ يَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2663 - Zurara narrated from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever intentionally eats saffron or food containing fragrance while in Ihram must offer a blood sacrifice (dam). However, if he does so out of forgetfulness, there is nothing upon him, but he should seek forgiveness from Allah (swt) and repent to Him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2664 - It was narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Ziyad, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "My servant poured water for me to perform ablution, and I was unaware that there was fragrance (dustashan) in it. I then washed my hands while I was in Ihram."
Imam (as) said: "Give something in charity as an expiation for that."
records.arabicText: 2664 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَضَّأَنِي اَلْغُلاَمُ وَ أَنَا لاَ أَعْلَمُ بدستشان فِيهِ طِيبٌ فَغَسَلْتُ يَدَيَّ وَ أَنَا مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ «تَصَدَّقْ بِشَيْءٍ لِذَلِكَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2664 - It was narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Ziyad, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"My servant poured water for me to perform ablution, and I was unaware that there was fragrance (dustashan) in it. I then washed my hands while I was in Ihram."
Imam (as) said: "Give something in charity as an expiation for that."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2665 - Ibrahim ibn Sufyan wrote to Abu al-Hasan (as) asking about a Muhrim washing his hands with soapwort (ushnan) that contains idhkhar (a type of fragrant grass).
Imam (as) wrote back: "I do not like it for you."
records.arabicText: 2665 - وَ كَتَبَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ سُفْيَانَ إِلَى أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : اَلْمُحْرِمُ يَغْسِلُ يَدَهُ بِأُشْنَانٍ فِيهِ اَلْإِذْخِرُ فَكَتَبَ «لاَ أُحِبُّهُ لَكَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2665 - Ibrahim ibn Sufyan wrote to Abu al-Hasan (as) asking about a Muhrim washing his hands with soapwort (ushnan) that contains idhkhar (a type of fragrant grass).
Imam (as) wrote back:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I do not like it for you."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2666 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
I asked him about a man who touched perfume while in Ihram, out of forgetfulness.
Imam (as) said: "He should wash his hands, recite the Talbiyah, and there is nothing upon him."
In another narration: "He should seek forgiveness from his Lord (azj)."
records.arabicText:
2666 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مَسَّ اَلطِّيبَ نَاسِياً وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ قَالَ «يَغْسِلُ يَدَيْهِ وَ يُلَبِّي وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ.
وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ: «وَ يَسْتَغْفِرُ رَبَّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2666 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked him about a man who touched perfume while in Ihram, out of forgetfulness.
Imam (as) said: "He should wash his hands, recite the Talbiyah, and there is nothing upon him."
In another narration: "He should seek forgiveness from his Lord (azj)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2667 - Humran narrated from Abu Ja'far (as) regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted:
"Then let them complete their prescribed duties and fulfill their vows" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:29).
Imam (as) said: "Tafath refers to a person's grooming, including the use of perfume. Once he has completed his rituals, perfume becomes permissible for him."
records.arabicText: 2667 - وَ رَوَى حُمْرَانُ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «۝ ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ وَ لْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ ۝» قَالَ «اَلتَّفَثُ حُفُوفُ اَلرَّجُلِ مِنَ اَلطِّيبِ فَإِذَا قَضَى نُسُكَهُ حَلَّ لَهُ اَلطِّيبُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2667 - Humran narrated from Abu Ja'far (as) regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted:
"Then let them complete their prescribed duties and fulfill their vows" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:29).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Tafath refers to a person's grooming, including the use of perfume. Once he has completed his rituals, perfume becomes permissible for him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2668 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdullah (as) about henna.
Imam (as) said: "A Muhrim may touch it and use it to treat his camel. It is not considered a perfume, and there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2668 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْحِنَّاءِ فَقَالَ «إِنَّ اَلْمُحْرِمَ لَيَمَسُّهُ وَ يُدَاوِي بِهِ بَعِيرَهُ وَ مَا هُوَ بِطِيبٍ وَ مَا بِهِ بَأْسٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2668 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdullah (as) about henna.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "A Muhrim may touch it and use it to treat his camel. It is not considered a perfume, and there is no harm in it."
